message: Node started: Implement the following algorithmic procedure to solve the Polyomino Mining problem: 1. State Representation: Grid State (G): An N x M matrix where each cell (r, c) is initialized to a state of {0, 1} (Unknown, Potential Oil). Polyomino Library (P): A set of shapes {S1, S2, ... Sk}, where each Si is a set of relative coordinates {(dr1, dc1), ...}. Candidate Placements (C): A set of tuples (Si, r, c) representing the possible placement of polyomino i with its reference point at (r, c). Initially, C contains all valid placements that fit within the grid boundaries. Occupied Cells (O): A set of coordinates representing cells confirmed to contain oil. 2. Query Logic and State Update: Aggregate Query: When a query is performed on a set of cells Q, the agent receives a value V (the count of oil cells in Q). Update Rule: For every placement p in C, calculate the intersection Ip = { cells in p } intersection Q. Pruning: A placement p is removed from C if the number of oil cells it would contribute to Q (i.e., |Ip|) is inconsistent with the query result V. Note: If the query is noisy, use a probabilistic threshold or a range [V-epsilon, V+epsilon] to prune. 3. Geometric Pruning Rules: Overlap Constraint: If two polyominoes p1 and p2 are known to be disjoint, any placement where p1 intersection p2 is not empty must be pruned. Boundary Constraint: Any placement p that results in cells outside the grid boundaries is discarded. Connectivity Constraint: If the problem specifies polyominoes must be connected or follow specific adjacency rules, prune placements that violate the topology of the known shapes. 4. Decision Criteria (Query vs. Guess): Calculate Information Gain (IG): For each possible subset of cells Q subset of G, calculate the expected reduction in the entropy of the set C. IG(Q) = H(C) - E[H(C | V)]. Calculate Guess Confidence: Let U be the set of cells covered by the union of all remaining placements in C. If C has converged such that all p in C result in the same set of occupied cells Ofinal, or if the cost of the next query Cquery exceeds the expected penalty of an incorrect guess Cerror * P(error), terminate. Execution Loop: a. If P(error) < Threshold, execute FINAL_ANSWER with the union
